title: Story Onboarding Theory — Ergonomic Entry, Familiar Anchors, and Rewarded Modeling
summary: Design doctrine for story openings and re-entry ramps: reduce cognitive load without reducing intrigue by using familiarity, clarity, and frequent reward as the three primary onboarding levers.

narrative.notes: Source document lives at docs/onboarding_theory.md. This theory applies wherever the reader must build or rebuild a mental model — people, setting, rules, stakes, tone, or pattern structure. It is not a recipe for Chapter One only; use it to evaluate any entry ramp, arc-opening, or re-entry point in the story. Core compressed target: Low-friction entry + clear focus + meaningful deltas + frequent reward = strong onboarding.
